title: 10 best herbal capsules for fever
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for fever are natural supplements that help alleviate and reduce body temperature during an illness. These capsules are made from various herbs that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to combat fever effectively. The best herbal capsules for fever include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Cinchona officinalis, Andrographis paniculata, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ephedra sinica, Sambucus nigra, Cinchona ledgeriana, and Eucalyptus globulus. If not treated, fever can lead to dehydration, fatigue, and decreased productivity, causing distress in daily life and negatively impacting work, social activities, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with fever because they contain the natural compound glycyrrhizin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This compound inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby reducing swelling and pain associated with fever. Additionally, glycyrrhizin exhibits antiviral properties, which help to combat viral infections that often cause fever. By reducing inflammation and fighting infection, herbal licorice capsules provide natural relief from fever symptoms, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fever by inhibiting the growth of viruses and bacteria that cause infection.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate symptoms of fever such as headaches, fatigue, and body aches, while also reducing inflammation in the body.

Saponins: These compounds have antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which help to fight off infections that cause fever, while also soothing the mucous membranes and reducing inflammation in the throat and respiratory tract.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties, which may help alleviate fever by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Flavonoids (including isoliquiritigenin and liquiritigenin): These phenolic compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antipyretic activities, which may contribute to the reduction of fever by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators.

Liquiritic acid: This phenolic compound has been reported to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate fever and associated discomfort by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and prostaglandins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are commonly used to make capsules for fever due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are also used to make capsules for fever due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with fever.

Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are commonly used to make capsules for fever due to their traditional use in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or cultivate them yourself.
Dry the harvested roots for 2 weeks in a low-temperature oven set at 50 degrees celsius.
Grind 200 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Mix the powdered roots with 500 grams of gelatin and 100 grams of water in a large mixing bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powdered herbal mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with fever because they contain the natural herb feverfew, which has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The active compound parthenolide in feverfew has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making it effective in reducing fever and relieving symptoms such as headache, body aches, and sweating. When taken as capsules, the parthenolide is quickly absorbed into the bloodstream, where it can help to lower the body's temperature and alleviate discomfort associated with fever.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide content: Feverfew capsules contain a compound called parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, helping to reduce fever and alleviate associated symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Feverfew's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by fever, reducing tissue damage and promoting recovery.
Vasoconstrictive effect: Feverfew's ability to constrict blood vessels helps to reduce blood flow to the skin, which can lead to a decrease in sweating and discomfort associated with fever.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: It helps with fever by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause fever, pain, and inflammation.
Caryophyllene oxide: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.
Chrysanthemumic acid: It exhibits antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties by inhibiting the production of cyclic AMP (cAMP), which plays a role in the regulation of body temperature.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Flowers: They are used for their ability to induce sweating, reduce fever, and alleviate headaches due to their volatile oil composition.
Roots: They are used for their antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing fever and alleviating associated symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency retention.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve its properties.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered plant material with a binding agent such as gelatin at a 1:5 ratio for 30 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with fever because they contain menthol, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as an herbal supplement, peppermint capsules can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a primary cause of fever. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effects can help to bring down the temperature and provide relief from symptoms such as sweating and chills. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective way to alleviate fevers without resorting to medication.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol in peppermint capsules helps to cool the skin, reducing heat and discomfort caused by fever. This cooling effect can provide relief from symptoms such as sweating, chills, and body aches.
Cineole: Cineole is a natural anti-inflammatory compound found in peppermint that can help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with fever, making it easier to breathe and relax.

Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is another key compound in peppermint that has natural decongestant properties, helping to loosen mucus and ease congestion caused by a fever, making it easier to expel phlegm from the body.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a natural coolant that helps to reduce body temperature and alleviate fever symptoms by stimulating the brain's temperature regulation center.
Limonene: Limonene has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate the discomfort associated with fever, while also boosting the immune system.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the severity of fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are commonly used to make capsules for fever due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to reduce body temperature and relieve symptoms.
Stems: Mentha x piperita stems are another commonly used part for making fever capsules, as they contain menthol and other compounds that help to soothe and calm the body.
Buds: Mentha x piperita buds are used to make fever capsules due to their high menthol content and other volatile oils that help to reduce fever and relieve congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried mentha x piperita leaves at peak potency for maximum flavor and aroma retention.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to preserve potency.
Grind 50g of the dried mentha x piperita leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed blender for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the ground powder with 90g of gelatin or vegetarian capsule material in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 200mg each of the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with fever because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. Echinacea, the main ingredient in Kansas coneflower, has been shown to reduce the severity and duration of fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and blocking the replication of viruses. Additionally, Kansas coneflower capsules have immunomodulatory effects that help to stimulate the immune system's natural response to infection, thereby reducing the body's temperature.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Kansas Coneflower contains compounds like cinnamic acid, caffeic acid, and ferulic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.

Immunomodulatory effects: The herb's polysaccharides and polyphenols enhance the body's natural immune response, helping to fight off infections that can cause fever, while also reducing the severity of symptoms.
Antiviral activity: Kansas Coneflower has been found to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that may be contributing to a fever, such as the common cold or flu.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, such as echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which help to reduce fever by modulating the body's response to infection.

Alkaloids (specifically, Cichoric Acid and Alkylamides): These compounds help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system, which in turn helps to alleviate fever and other symptoms associated with infection.

Polyphenols (specifically, Kaempferol and Quercetin): Polyphenols have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with fever, ultimately alleviating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make fever capsules due to their high concentration of alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: They are also used to make fever capsules due to their ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce fever.
Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make fever capsules because they contain isobutylamides, which have been shown to have antipyretic properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of echinacea angustifolia roots, dried to 5 percent moisture content in a low-temperature dryer for 2 hours.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a grinder at a speed of 5000 rpm for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered roots with 20 grams of gelatin to make a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the echinacea angustifolia powder, using a capsule filler machine at a rate of 50 capsules per minute.
Package the herbal capsules in airtight containers and store them in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with fever because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called gossypol, which has been shown to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever. The capsule's extract also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the body against oxidative stress caused by infection or inflammation. Additionally, the Maidenhair tree capsules' bioactive compounds have immunomodulatory effects, which can help to boost the immune system's natural response to illness, further reducing the duration and severity of fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Maidenhair tree capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as pain and swelling.
Antipyretic properties: The capsules contain antipyretic compounds that help to lower body temperature and relieve the discomfort associated with high fevers.

Immune-boosting activity: Maidenhair tree capsules have immunomodulatory effects that help to enhance the body's natural defenses against infection, reducing the severity and duration of fever-causing illnesses.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the severity of fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to mitigate the negative effects of fever by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Icariin: This flavonoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties.
Seeds: They are often used due to their content of ginkgolic acid and bilobalide, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing effects.
Barks: They are sometimes used as they are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which may contribute to their potential anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves by selecting mature green leaves from healthy trees in late summer early fall.
Dry ginkgo biloba leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper trays and allowing air to circulate for 24 hours.
Grind dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mill or coffee grinder at a dosage of 250mg per capsule.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba powder using a powder filling machine or spoon at a dosage of 250mg per capsule.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties, which work to reduce body temperature. The herb's active compounds, such as chamazulene and apigenin, have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are responsible for causing inflammation and pain associated with fever. By reducing these inflammatory responses, yarrow capsules help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, body aches, and sweating, providing natural relief from fever-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Yarrow capsules have been shown to reduce body temperature by dilating blood vessels and increasing sweating, helping to alleviate fever symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in yarrow capsules inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and alleviating associated pain and discomfort.
Antibacterial properties: Yarrow capsules contain compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for many infections, helping to combat the underlying cause of fever.
remedy_constituents: Caffeic acid: As a phenolic compound, caffeic acid has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate fever symptoms.
Apigenin: This flavonoid compound has been shown to have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to decrease fever and alleviate symptoms associated with inflammation.
Bornyl acetate: As a sesquiterpene, bornyl acetate has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which contribute to its ability to reduce fever and alleviate inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for fever due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules for fever due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds, which have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: They are used to make capsules for fever due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 25 grams of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 5 grams of dried achillea millefolium into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill gelatin capsules with approximately 0.5 grams of the powdered herb.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties. The herb contains a compound called linalool, which has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with fever. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by fever, promoting relaxation and restful sleep. By taking English lavender capsules, individuals may experience relief from fever-related discomfort and faster recovery times.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lavender's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to fever, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting cells from damage.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool, may help to reduce swelling and pain associated with fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Calming and sedative properties: Lavender's calming and sedative effects can help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are common symptoms of fever, allowing the body to focus on fighting off the underlying infection or illness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps with fever by exerting anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can reduce the severity of fever and alleviate associated symptoms.

Linalyl acetate: A terpene that contributes to the antipyretic (fever-reducing) effects of Lavandula angustifolia by acting as a potent inhibitor of inflammation and oxidative stress, which can help to bring down fever.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain essential oils that help to reduce fever and promote relaxation.
Flowers: They are rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, which have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate fever.
Seeds: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids that contribute to their antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for fever relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of their fragrance and potency.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Mix the dried flowers with 100 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of glycerin to create the capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture using a capsule filling machine at 200 capsules per minute.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the body's temperature. The eucalyptus oil present in the capsules has a cooling effect that alleviates symptoms such as headache and fatigue often associated with fever. Additionally, the antiviral and antibacterial properties of Tasmanian blue gum work together to combat the underlying infection, reducing the severity and duration of fever. This natural approach provides relief from discomfort and promotes overall well-being during periods of illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains anti-inflammatory compounds like eucalyptol and cineole, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headache and body aches.

Expectorant properties: The capsules contain expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughs often associated with fever.

Antimicrobial activity: Tasmanian Blue Gum has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various microorganisms, including bacteria and viruses, which can help combat infections that may be causing the fever.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): As a potent decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent, cineole helps to relieve fever by reducing the production of mucus and inflammation in the respiratory tract.
α-Pinene: This terpene acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby reducing the body temperature and alleviating fever symptoms.
Limonene: With its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, limonene helps to combat infections and reduce fever by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and modulating the immune response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Eucalyptus globulus due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compound.
Barks: Containing a high amount of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to their medicinal properties and help in reducing fever.
Stems: Also rich in eucalyptol, which aids in relieving congestion and fever due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants at dawn to minimize moisture content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 40°c for 2 hours to prevent over-drying.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix 1g of the powder with 1g of gelatin or vegetarian capsule filler in a small bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with fever because they contain active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the body, alleviate pain and discomfort associated with fever, and even lower body temperature by stimulating sweat production. Additionally, ginger's natural antimicrobial properties can help combat underlying infections that may be causing the fever. By taking herbal ginger capsules regularly, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and symptoms of fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with fevers, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal ginger capsules' antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress caused by the infection or virus responsible for the fever, which can aid in reducing the severity of symptoms.

Vasodilation and sweating: Ginger's vasodilatory effects help increase blood flow to the skin, promoting sweat production, which is an important mechanism for the body to naturally cool down and regulate its temperature when experiencing a fever.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce the severity of fever by alleviating pain and discomfort associated with it.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that contribute to reducing fever by easing pain and discomfort.
6-Gingerol: This compound has been shown to have antipyretic properties, which help directly reduce fever by acting on the body's thermoregulatory centers.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Dried and powdered rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale are used to make capsules for fever due to their active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties.
Roots: The dried and powdered roots of Zingiber officinale are used for their ability to reduce fever and relieve nausea, thanks to the presence of gingerols and shogaols.
Buds: Ginger buds are used to make capsules for fever due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the zingiber officinale roots by digging up the mature rhizomes in the fall season when they are fully mature.
Clean and dry the harvested roots to remove any excess moisture by using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder at high speed for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered zingiber officinale root with a binding agent such as gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a ratio of 1 part root to 2 parts binder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule filler machine or a manual capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with fever because of its unique compound curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. When taken as a supplement, curcumin is absorbed into the bloodstream, where it can help to reduce body temperature by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory compounds that contribute to fever. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals, further reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. As a result, turmeric capsules have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections characterized by high fever.
remedy_properties: C Curcumin: A powerful anti-inflammatory compound found in turmeric, curcumin helps reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and molecules.
Antioxidant Properties: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to a reduced risk of fever development and severity.

Cytokine Modulation: Turmeric's bioactive compounds, such as curcuminoids, have been shown to modulate cytokines (proteins that regulate immune responses), helping to alleviate fever by reducing excessive inflammation and immune activation.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds help reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the development and maintenance of fever.
Tumerones: These sesquiterpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate fever by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): This curcuminoid also possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: the roots of Curcuma longa are used to make capsules for fever as they contain a lower concentration of curcuminoids than rhyzomes, but still provide therapeutic benefits.
Leaves: Leaves: leaves of Curcuma longa may be used to make capsules for fever, although they are less commonly used and contain lower levels of curcuminoids than rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried curcuma longa root powder from a clean and dry place.
Weigh out 1 gram of gelatin and soak it in 20 milliliters of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Combine the gelatin mixture with 20 milliliters of glycerin and 10 milliliters of purified water in a bowl.
Add the dried curcuma longa root powder to the bowl and mix well for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with fever because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with fevers. By reducing inflammation, frankincense capsules can help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and joint pain that are commonly experienced during fever. Additionally, the anti-pyretic properties of frankincense help to lower body temperature, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Frankincense has anti-inflammatory compounds like boswellic acids, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with fever, helping to alleviate symptoms such as headaches and body aches.

Antipyretic property: Frankincense has natural antipyretic properties that help reduce the body's temperature, making it an effective herbal remedy for relieving fever symptoms like chills and sweating.
Immunomodulatory property: Frankincense contains immunomodulators that can enhance the immune system's response to infection, helping to fight off underlying causes of fever and promote recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, frankincense roots have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating associated discomfort.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of frankincense plants contain volatile oils that help to cool the body and relieve symptoms of fever.
remedy_recipe: Dry freshly harvested boswellia serrata leaves in a clean room at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried boswellia serrata leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered boswellia serrata with 20 grams of excipients like silica or calcium carbonate to fill capsules accurately.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400mg of the prepared herbal blend and seal them tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with fever because they contain antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with a fever. The herb elder has been traditionally used for centuries to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections, which often cause fevers. The active compounds in elder capsules, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, work together to boost the immune system and help bring down body temperature, providing relief from symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and chills.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Elderberry's antiviral properties help to combat viral infections that cause fever by inhibiting the replication of viruses and reducing their ability to infect cells.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and anthocyanins, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate symptoms associated with fever such as headaches and joint pain.
Mucilages: The mucilage content in elderberry helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycosides and alkaloids, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
Barks: Containing triterpenes and polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the body during a fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves on a sunny day in summer when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm place for two days to remove excess moisture then store it in an airtight container.
Grind 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle taking about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 50 grams of pure gelatin and 100 milliliters of boiling water to create a smooth paste leaving it to cool for 30 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with one-eighth teaspoon or 250 milligrams of the herbal paste and seal them immediately storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with fever because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The herb, Calendula officinalis, contains flavonoids and triterpenoids that work synergistically to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever. Additionally, the capsules have been shown to inhibit the release of pro-inflammatory mediators, thus preventing excessive inflammation. This natural remedy can provide relief from fever-induced discomfort and promote a swift recovery, making it an effective alternative to conventional medications.
remedy_properties: Calendic acid: This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties, making it effective in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with colds and flu.

Neryl acetate: This terpene has been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activity, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with fever, as well as ease congestion and coughing.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in pot marigold capsules have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat viral infections that cause fever, while also providing antioxidant benefits that support overall health and wellbeing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of pot marigold have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective in reducing fever.
Seeds: With their high content of essential fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds of pot marigold help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers and clean them thoroughly of debris.
Mix one teaspoon of the cleaned calendula petals with two cups of boiling water to create a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes then strain the liquid and discard the solids.
Combine the cooled decoction with glycerin in a ratio of one part decoction to three parts glycerin.
Fill gelatin capsules with the resulting calendula-glycerin mixture and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with fever because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that help to calm irritated tissues in the throat, chest, and bronchial passages. When taken as a capsule or tea, marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with fever. Additionally, its demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, helping to soothe and quiet coughs, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving fever-related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and coat the mucous membranes in the throat, nose, and respiratory tract, helping to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with fever.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and pain caused by fever-related infections, such as sinusitis or bronchitis, making it easier for the body to recover.

Antiviral activity: Marshmallow root has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help to combat viral infections that cause fever, such as the common cold or flu, by inhibiting the replication of viruses and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marshmallow roots have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing fever and relieving body aches.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried marshmallow root (althaea officinalis) from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground marshmallow root powder with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids for 20 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams each of the cooled herbal infusion and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound capsules helps with fever because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, which in turn reduces inflammation and eases congestion. The capsules' active compounds, such as maronic acid and saponins, work together to ease coughs and relieve chest congestion, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and heat. By targeting the root cause of fever, horehound capsules help to alleviate symptoms and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic: Horehound contains compounds like marrubiin, which helps reduce fever by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, leading to decreased body temperature.

Anti-inflammatory: The alkaloids present in horehound, such as amarogentin and frangulin, possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with fever, including pain and swelling.
Expectorant: Horehound's natural expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of alkaloids, including marrubium and asclepiadin, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective in reducing fever.

Barks: The barks are used due to their rich content of glycosides, such as horehoundin, which have been shown to exhibit antispasmodic and sedative effects, helping to alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marrubium vulgare leaves and dry them in the shade for 3-4 days.
Crush 30 grams of dried marrubium vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered marrubium vulgare with 50 grams of glycerin and 10 grams of water to form a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the paste and seal them tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with fever because they contain thymol, a natural compound that has antipyretic properties. When taken internally, thymol helps to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to a decrease in body temperature. Additionally, thymol's antimicrobial effects help to combat infections that can cause fevers. By targeting both the underlying infection and the symptoms of fever, herbal thyme capsules can provide effective relief from feverish conditions.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol has antibacterial and antiviral properties that help to combat the underlying infection causing the fever, reducing its severity and duration.
Cineole: Cineole is a natural decongestant that helps to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure associated with fevers, making it easier to breathe and relieving discomfort.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the body's response to infection, alleviating symptoms such as headache, fatigue, and body aches commonly associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce fever by combating infection-causing pathogens.
Barks: Thyme barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that possess antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate fever symptoms by reducing body temperature.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in sufficient quantities.
Dry the plant material thoroughly using air circulation or low-temperature drying methods.
Grind the dried thymus vulgaris into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part ground thymus vulagaris with 2 parts carrier herb like oat straw and fill sterile capsules with the blend.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram capsules helps with fever because they contain a compound called thymol, which has natural antipyretic properties. Thymol works by increasing sweating, which helps to lower body temperature and relieve symptoms of fever. Additionally, the herb's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for the body to expel toxins and reduce congestion. By addressing these underlying causes of fever, wild marjoram capsules can provide natural relief and support the body's recovery process.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Wild marjoram capsules have been traditionally used to reduce fever due to their ability to lower body temperature, making them an effective natural remedy for treating fevers.
Anti-inflammatory property: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, joint pain, and muscle aches associated with fever, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant property: Wild marjoram's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by fever, which can lead to tissue damage and other complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective in reducing fever by soothing inflammation and promoting sweating.
Rhyzomes: Containing volatile oils with analgesic and antipyretic properties, rhyzomes help to calm down the body's response to fever and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried origanum vulgare leaves and flowers from trusted sources.
Grind 2 tablespoons of the harvested material into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the powdered origanum vulgare with 500mg of fillers like silicon dioxide or calcium carbonate.
Encapsulate the mixture in vegetarian capsules with a size of #0 using capsule machine.
Store the prepared capsules in airtight containers at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with fever because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat microbial infections that can cause fever. Additionally, sage's calming effects on the body may also help to soothe a sore throat and ease congestion, making it a natural remedy for relieving symptoms associated with fever.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Herbal sage capsules contain compounds that can help reduce body temperature, relieving symptoms of fever, such as sweating and chills. This is because sage's essential oils, like camphor and borneol, have a cooling effect on the body.

Anti-inflammatory property: Sage's bioactive compounds, including rosmarinic acid, can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with fever. By reducing inflammation, herbal sage capsules may help alleviate symptoms of fever, such as headaches and muscle aches.

Antioxidant property: Herbal sage capsules are rich in antioxidants, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, that can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by fevers. This property may contribute to sage's ability to reduce the severity and duration of fever-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in bioactive compounds, including triterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective in reducing fever.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of sage contains volatile oils and phenolic acids that possess antimicrobial and analgesic activities, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever such as pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves measure 20g of dried or 40g of fresh herb.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a soft-bristled brush.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ground herb powder with 500ml of boiling distilled water to create an infusion steeping for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle filling 100-capsule-sized gelatin capsules with the liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with fever because of its natural antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of a fever. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various viruses and bacteria that can cause fever. Additionally, lemon balm's ability to soothe the nervous system and promote relaxation can help alleviate symptoms such as sweating, chills, and restlessness often associated with fever. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to manage fever without harsh medications.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Lemon balm's antiviral properties help to reduce the severity and duration of viral infections, such as the common cold and flu, which often cause fever.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to fever reduction and relief from associated symptoms like headaches and sore throats.
Antipyretic properties: Lemon balm's antipyretic properties help to lower body temperature by increasing sweating and promoting heat dissipation, providing natural relief from fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and relieving associated symptoms.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain rosmarinic acid, which has antioxidant and antiviral properties that can help combat infection and alleviate fever-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves ensuring no signs of contamination or mold.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm well-ventilated area for 24 hours without direct sunlight.
Grind dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of powdered melissa officinalis with 50 milliliters of vegetable oil and let it infuse for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth and fill capsules with 250 milligram dose for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with fever because of its natural antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the body's muscles and reduce inflammation. The herb is also known for its ability to calm the nervous system, which can help to alleviate feelings of anxiety and restlessness that often accompany fever. Additionally, valerian has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat underlying infections that may be causing the fever.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Valerian root has a natural cooling effect on the body, which helps to reduce fever by promoting sweating and dilating blood vessels, thus bringing down the body temperature.

Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant compounds help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by infections, reducing the severity of symptoms such as inflammation and pain associated with fever.

Sedative and anti-inflammatory properties: The sedative properties of valerian root can help reduce restlessness and irritability often associated with fever, while its anti-inflammatory effects may alleviate headaches, muscle aches, and joint pains that come with fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and alkaloids, valerian roots are believed to have a calming effect on the body, helping to reduce fever symptoms such as sweating and discomfort.

Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the valerian plant, rhyzomes contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate fever-related inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and dry them for at least two weeks in a warm dark place.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered valeriana officinalis with three parts of organic cornstarch to prevent clumping and improve flow.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with approximately 200-250 milligrams of the prepared valeriana mixture and seal them tightly.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fever that are treated with herbal capsules are various, including headaches, migraines, and sinusitis. Herbal remedies such as ginger, turmeric, and eucalyptus are commonly used to alleviate symptoms like pain, inflammation, and congestion. Other conditions, like arthritis, muscle aches, and menstrual cramps, also respond well to herbal supplements like willow bark, devil's claw, and cramp bark.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with fever is a blend of Echinacea, Ashwagandha, and Ginger. Echinacea enhances the immune system, reducing the duration and severity of fever. Ashwagandha aids in stress reduction and promotes overall well-being. Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate discomfort and soothe the body. This synergistic trio supports the body's natural healing process, providing relief from fever-related symptoms. Consult a healthcare professional before combining herbal supplements.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with fever because they contain compounds that stimulate the immune system, producing white blood cells to fight off invading pathogens. The herb's active ingredients, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce the severity of fever. Additionally, Echinacea's ability to activate the body's natural killer cells helps to eliminate infected cells, reducing the duration and intensity of fever. This natural response supports the body's recovery from illness.
remedy_constituents: Ishogankacin: Acts as an immunomodulator to stimulate the immune system, which can help reduce fever and alleviate its symptoms.
Cichoric acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the inflammation associated with fever, and also has antioxidant properties that can reduce oxidative stress.
Caffeic acid: Has antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce fever and alleviate its symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are considered the most potent part of the plant, rich in medicinal compounds.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing fever.
Flowers: They contain alkylamides and other compounds that have immunomodulatory effects, helping the body fight off infections that cause fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20% of the echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and the flowers have faded.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in a temperature-controlled environment at 35-40°c for 7-10 hours.
Crush the dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powdered echinacea purpurea with 10 parts of a filler material such as cornstarch or tapioca starch using a 1:10 ratio.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5-1 gram of the echinacea purpurea mixture and seal the capsules using a heat sealer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with fever because they contain the active compound gingerol, a natural anti-inflammatory agent. Gingerol works by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the development of fever. Additionally, gingerol's warming properties help to increase blood circulation, which helps to dissipate heat and reduce body temperature. As a result, gingerol in Zingiber officinale capsules provides relief from fever and other associated symptoms, such as aches and pains, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Helps with fever by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby lowering body temperature.
Shogaol: Assists in fever reduction by acting as a natural antipyretic, reducing the production of heat-producing compounds in the body.
Zingibain: Contributes to fever alleviation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Roots are used as a substitute for rhyzomes in some cases, as they also contain gingerols and shogaols, although in lower concentrations.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used, although less frequently than rhyzomes and roots, as they contain smaller amounts of gingerols and shogaols.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the morning when the dew is still present.
Wash the roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove any impurities or debris.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 3-4 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 5000 rpm.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powder and seal them using a capsule sealer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with fever because they contain quinine, a natural compound that has been used for centuries to treat malaria and reduce body temperature. This herb works by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are chemical messengers that trigger fever. Quinine in Cinchona officinalis capsules also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of fever and alleviate symptoms such as headaches and body aches. Regular use of Cinchona officinalis capsules can provide relief from fever and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps reduce fever by inhibiting the growth and replication of malaria parasites, which can cause fever, and also by modulating the body's temperature regulation.
Quinidine: It helps to reduce fever by acting as a vasodilator, which helps to increase blood flow and promote sweating, thereby reducing body temperature.

Cinchonine: It helps to reduce fever by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to reduce the inflammation and swelling associated with fever, and also by modulating the body's immune response to fight off infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the primary source of quinine, a key ingredient used to treat malaria and fever.
Barks: The barks contain high amounts of quinine and other alkaloids, making them a common ingredient in fever-reducing capsules.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cinchona officinalis contain quinine and other medicinal compounds, which are often extracted and used in fever-reducing capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark from a trusted supplier or cultivated source.
Dry the bark further by spreading it out in a single layer on a paper bag for 2 hours.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250-500 milligrams of the powdered bark with 100-200 milligrams of a neutral-tasting carrier powder for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata capsules helps with fever because they contain the herb Andrographis, a natural anti-inflammatory and immune-booster that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. It contains compounds like andrographolide, which helps to reduce the severity and duration of fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. By regulating the body's inflammatory response, Andrographis paniculata capsules can help to alleviate symptoms of fever and promote a faster recovery, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, flu, and other illnesses.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: This compound helps reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators, which contribute to the body's inflammatory response during fever.
Neoandrographolide: It assists in alleviating fever by possessing anti-inflammatory properties, which help regulate the body's immune response and reduce the severity of fever.

Dihydroandrographolide: This constituent helps with fever by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation triggered by fever.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Andrographis paniculata are the most commonly used part for making capsules due to their high content of andrographolide, a key bioactive compound.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used to make capsules, containing a significant amount of andrographolide and other beneficial compounds.
Roots: The roots of Andrographis paniculata are sometimes used to make capsules, although less commonly than the leaves and stems, as they contain a lower concentration of andrographolide.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried andrographis paniculata leaves and stems from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Grind 250g of the dried andrographis paniculata into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 100g of the ground andrographis paniculata powder with 50g of gelatin capsules in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the andrographis paniculata mixture using a small spoon for 10 minutes.
Label and store the 100 filled andrographis paniculata capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with fever because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to fever. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood circulation and oxygenation may help the body's natural processes to fight off the underlying infection, thereby reducing the severity of the fever. This natural remedy can provide relief and support the body's recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce fever by acting as potent antioxidants that protect the body from oxidative stress, thereby alleviating inflammation associated with fever.
Bilobalide: Assist in lowering fever by modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, making it an effective compound in fever reduction.
Ginkgolides: Help to alleviate fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory substances, such as cytokines and prostaglandins, which contribute to fever development.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba for fever capsules is the leaves, due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are also used in fever capsules, as they contain a compound called ginkgotoxin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects.
Buds: The buds of the Ginkgo biloba plant are used in some fever capsules, as they are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in early fall when they are fully grown and have turned golden yellow.
Dry the leaves in a dehydrator at 95f for 8 hours or in a shaded area for 1 week.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of ginkgo biloba powder with 1 gram of cornstarch and 1/4 gram of gelatin in a bowl.
Encapsulate the mixture in empty gelatin capsules and seal them using a capsule filler for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with fever because they contain licorice root, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes the body and reduces inflammation. This herb has been traditionally used to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can often exacerbate fever. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that cause fever. By addressing the root causes of fever, these capsules can help alleviate symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce fever by its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which suppress the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reduce body temperature.
Licoricidin: Exhibits antiviral and antipyretic activities, helping to decrease fever by inhibiting the replication of fever-causing viruses and reducing inflammation.
Licochalcone A: Displays antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to the reduction of fever by scavenging free radicals and mitigating the inflammatory response.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties that helps reduce fever.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in traditional medicine to treat fever and other health issues due to their antipyretic and analgesic properties.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for fever reduction.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a filler powder such as starch or cellulose in a ratio of 1:2.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill empty vegetarian capsules with the powder mixture for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool and dry place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica capsules helps with fever because they contain ephedrine and other bioactive compounds that stimulate the body's natural thermogenesis. This process increases heat production, helping to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. The herb also possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting a swift recovery. By boosting the body's immune response, Ephedra sinica capsules provide relief from fever and promote overall well-being. Regular use can also help prevent future illness.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help to reduce fever by increasing sweating, which helps to lower body temperature.
Amino acids (e.g. ephedrine): Ephedrine helps to reduce fever by stimulating the body's natural response to infection, increasing heart rate and circulation to fight off the infection.
Terpenoids (e.g. isoborneol): Terpenoids like isoborneol help to reduce fever by cooling the body and reducing inflammation caused by the fever.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their ephedrine content, which helps to reduce fever and relieve respiratory issues.
Rhyzomes: They are used because they contain ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which help to alleviate fever and congestion.
Stems: They are used due to their high ephedrine content, which helps to reduce fever and relieve respiratory symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ephedra sinica roots and stems in early spring when the plant is still dormant for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested ephedra sinica plant material in a warm area with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried ephedra sinica plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 to 500 milligrams of the ephedra sinica powder with 100 to 200 milligrams of filler material like starch or cellulose.
Encapsulate the ephedra sinica and filler mixture in gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine or a manual capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra capsules helps with fever because they contain the active compounds found in elderberry flowers, which have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds, including anthocyanins and flavonoids, work to reduce the severity and duration of fever by inhibiting the replication of viruses and reducing inflammation in the body. By using Sambucus nigra capsules, individuals may experience relief from fever and associated symptoms, such as headaches and fatigue, allowing for a faster recovery and return to normal health.
remedy_constituents: Isovitexin: It helps with fever by having anti-inflammatory properties, which reduces the body's response to fever and eases symptoms.
Isoorientin: It helps with fever by exhibiting antiviral and antioxidant properties, which helps to combat the underlying infection causing fever.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): They help with fever by having anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which reduces the body's temperature and eases symptoms of fever.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make capsules for fever relief due to their high content of antiviral and antimicrobial compounds.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for fever relief due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Fruits: They are used to make capsules for fever relief due to their high content of vitamins and antioxidants that help reduce fever.
remedy_recipe: Collect 250 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from a reputable supplier or harvest them responsibly.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered sambucus nigra with 50 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of water to create a uniform mixture in 10 minutes.
Fill 100-200 capsule shells with the mixture using a powder filling machine or spoon for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona ledgeriana capsules helps with fever because they contain quinine, a natural compound extracted from the bark of the Cinchona tree. Quinine has been used for centuries to treat malaria, which often causes high fever. Its antipyretic properties help reduce body temperature by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and interleukins, which contribute to fever. Additionally, quinine's ability to relax blood vessels and improve circulation may also aid in cooling the body down and alleviating symptoms associated with fever.
remedy_constituents: Quinic Acid: Quinic Acid has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce fever by alleviating the underlying causes of body heat.
Quinine Alkaloid: Quinine Alkaloid has a strong antipyretic effect, which directly helps to lower body temperature and reduce fever.
Quinovine Alkaloid: Quinovine Alkaloid also has antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with high body temperature.
remedy_parts: Barks: They contain quinine, a key alkaloid responsible for the plant's antimalarial and fever-reducing properties.
Leaves: They also contain quinine and other alkaloids that contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
Stems: Like the barks and leaves, the stems of Cinchona ledgeriana contain quinine and other alkaloids that make it useful for fever treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of cinchona ledgeriana bark from mature trees in a controlled environment with minimal contamination.
Dry the harvested bark in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to preserve its medicinal properties.
Crush the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to facilitate even extraction.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powdered bark with 2 tablespoons of glycerin and 2 tablespoons of distilled water to create a uniform liquid extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1/2 teaspoon of the extract mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with fever because they contain the essential oil eucalyptol, which has potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. When taken orally, eucalyptol is absorbed into the bloodstream, where it can help to reduce fever and alleviate the symptoms of respiratory infections, such as coughs and sore throats. Eucalyptus globulus capsules also have an expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It helps with fever by acting as a decongestant and antipyretic agent, reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues associated with fever.
Cineole: It helps with fever by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing the body's inflammatory response and alleviating fever symptoms.

α-pinene: It helps with fever by exhibiting antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections causing fever, as well as by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are widely used in herbal medicine due to their high oil content, particularly eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus contains various compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in treating fever and other respiratory issues.
Buds: The buds of Eucalyptus globulus are also used in herbal medicine due to their high eucalyptol content, which helps to relieve fever, congestion, and other respiratory problems.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and 100 grams of gelatin capsules for the capsules.
Grind 150 grams of the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a mill for 10 minutes.
Mix 50 grams of the ground powder with 50 grams of vegetable oil in a 1000ml glass bottle for 5 minutes.
Fill 100 grams of the gelatin capsules with the eucalyptus globulus powder mixture and seal the capsules immediately.
Label and store the herbal eucalyptus globulus capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.
